hyperedges.label: Pocket Mint Design System Documentation (light current + dark legacy)

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: AGENTS.md Agent Instructions
nodes.rationale: Warns that this Next.js version has breaking changes vs training data and directs agents to read node_modules/next/dist/docs before writing code.

nodes.label: Pocket Mint Frontend Audit Report
nodes.rationale: Full-codebase audit rating overall quality 5/10 and risk High; concludes no new features until identity boundary, route protection, fabricated data, and command/preview integrity are fixed.

nodes.label: Finding: Client-Spoofable API Identity Boundary
nodes.rationale: Browser-set x-user-id/x-user-email headers plus a public shared x-api-key let any user claim another Supabase UID; rated the product's highest (P0 Critical) risk. Recommended fix: verified Supabase bearer JWT with user derived from the sub claim.

nodes.label: Finding: Fabricated Financial Trends and Composition
nodes.rationale: Dashboard hardcodes +4.2% vs last month, wallet summary invents previous net worth as netWorth*0.958 with a manufactured sparkline, and installment liability assumes an unsupported 70/30 principal/interest split — invented performance presented as real account history.
nodes.norm_label: finding: fabricated financial trends and composition

nodes.label: Finding: Incomplete Authenticated Route Protection
nodes.rationale: Middleware guards only /dashboard while /wallets, /transactions, /cicilan, and /profile share the authenticated (app) shell unguarded; recommends protecting the whole (app) surface or asserting auth in the server layout.

nodes.label: Frontend Authentication Doc — Supabase Bearer JWT
nodes.norm_label: frontend authentication doc — supabase bearer jwt

nodes.label: Supabase Bearer JWT Authentication Model
nodes.rationale: JWT-only auth replaces the spoofable client-asserted identity headers (x-api-key/x-user-id/x-user-email); backend verifies signature, issuer, audience, expiry and derives the user exclusively from the verified sub claim. Token is read fresh from the Supabase session on every request, never cached at module scope.

nodes.label: 401 Single-Retry-With-Fresh-Token Policy
nodes.rationale: A 401 means the backend rejected the request before acting on it, so one retry with a refreshed token is safe even for mutations; a same-token 401 clears auth state and redirects to /login to avoid retry loops.

nodes.label: /users/sync Provisioning Flow
nodes.rationale: Sends only {name} with the Bearer token; backend uses the verified JWT sub as the local user id. Idempotent sync on every password login self-heals deferred signups; sync is non-blocking and failures never block auth.

nodes.label: Corporate/Modern Design System
nodes.rationale: Targets the self-hosting enthusiast valuing privacy and precision; aims for 'controlled transparency' — data density and legibility on an off-white canvas with functional whitespace instead of decoration.

nodes.label: Logic-Gated Color Palette
nodes.rationale: Hue itself carries ledger semantics: mint green reserved for assets/income/growth, deep slate for stability and navigation, amber for pending/warning states, coral red for debt and over-budget alerts.

nodes.label: Design Tokens (globals.css @theme)
nodes.rationale: app/globals.css @theme is the source of truth; only semantic token classes are valid — Tailwind default palettes (slate/gray/zinc/indigo/emerald/rose) are rejected by a hook, and raw hex silently breaks if the theme shifts.

nodes.label: Financial Display Rules
nodes.rationale: All financial figures use JetBrains Mono (font-mono), positive values text-primary with + prefix, negative/debt text-destructive, net worth minimum text-4xl font-mono font-bold, Rp prefix lighter than the number.

nodes.label: Sidebar Rules (hover-expand rail)
nodes.rationale: Active nav item uses text-primary font-semibold + aria-current, not a background fill; the border-l active style from older mocks is explicitly superseded. Desktop rail 60px collapsed to 300px expanded via absolute overlay that never reflows the page.

nodes.label: Wallet Types: ASSET vs DEBT
nodes.rationale: DEBT outstanding is stored as a NEGATIVE balance and displayed as Math.abs(balance) in red; spendable for debt wallets is creditLimit - |balance|, for assets it is the balance itself.

nodes.label: Net Worth Calculation
nodes.rationale: netWorth = sum of ASSET balances only; debt does not subtract directly because assets shrink when the repayment transaction happens (decided Jul 2026, implemented in backend calculateNetWorth, dashboard, wallets page). Always computed live, never stored.

nodes.label: Debt Ratio Thresholds
nodes.rationale: debtRatio = totalDebt / totalCreditLimit * 100; <30% Aman (green), 30-60% Hati-hati (orange), >60% Bahaya (red).

nodes.label: Installment Model A
nodes.rationale: Only from a DEBT wallet as EXPENSE; grand total (principal + flat interest) locked at creation by the backend. Valid tenors 3/6/12 months (backend VALID_TENORS, frontend TENORS kept in sync); progress = paidMonths/totalMonths; cannot be deleted mid-progress.

nodes.label: Paylater Rates Precedence
nodes.rationale: Wallet-stored rates win over provider presets; presets come from a backend endpoint via usePaylaterRates() name-matched to the wallet — preset constants must never be hardcoded in components.

nodes.label: Transaction Rules (INCOME/EXPENSE/TRANSFER)
nodes.rationale: Debt repayment is modeled as a TRANSFER from an asset wallet into the debt wallet (negative balance increments toward 0) rather than a separate backend type; e-wallets cannot pay CC/paylater bills — PAY DEBT sources are BANK/CASH only.

nodes.label: Agent Rules Skill (legacy .claude/skills)
nodes.rationale: Defines skill load order (agent-rules, then ui-system, then financial-logic), backend-before-frontend sequencing, {data,error} API envelope, and completion criteria requiring clean build and docs/audit.md updates.

nodes.label: Git & PR Conventions (dev=Staging, master=Production)
nodes.rationale: Every task starts a new feature/fix branch from dev; never commit or push directly to dev or master; PRs must target dev, never master except an explicit user-ordered production hotfix.

nodes.label: docs/audit.md Maintenance Rule
nodes.rationale: Any page/component change under apps/frontend/app/ obliges an immediate update of the docs/audit.md feature map (routes, main components, parent-child layout relations).

nodes.label: Pro-Fintech Dark Design System (legacy)
nodes.rationale: High-Contrast Modern dark theme on Deep Charcoal #131313 with mint #4ade80 hero; YAML frontmatter is the raw Material-token export from Stitch AI, and where it conflicts with app/globals.css @theme, globals.css wins.

nodes.label: Inset Elevation Model
nodes.rationale: Depth via borders + tonal layering instead of light-source shadows: cards sit darker than the floor (#0e0e0e vs #131313) and are defined by 1px #262626 borders, mirroring premium terminal software; shadows only appear on Level 2+ as a soft deep-black glow.

nodes.label: UI System Skill (legacy dark variant)
nodes.rationale: Dark-token variant: 176px fixed sidebar with border-l-2 #4ade80 active state and arbitrary-value hex classes — conventions later superseded by the light-theme skill's semantic-token and text-primary active rules.

nodes.label: Master Prompt — Session Task List
nodes.rationale: Ordered session plan: fix backend EADDRINUSE :::5001 port conflict first, then wallet CRUD, then active installments on dashboard, then monthly P&L reset — confirming API shape and no regressions after each step.

nodes.label: Task: Wallet CRUD Endpoints
nodes.rationale: POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /wallets; net worth must be recalculated after every wallet mutation, DEBT wallets require creditLimit, responses return updated wallet + new net worth.

nodes.label: Task: Active Installments on Dashboard
nodes.rationale: Dashboard shows installments where paidMonths < totalMonths via GET /installments?status=active — must come from the live DB, never hardcoded or mocked.

nodes.label: Task: Monthly P&L Reset
nodes.rationale: GET /transactions/summary?month=YYYY-MM returns {income, expenses, netSavings} scoped to the calendar month; dashboard widget recalculates when the month changes, no static values.

nodes.label: dev Skill — Start Dev Servers (.claude copy)
nodes.rationale: No root script starts both apps; backend (Express + Prisma, ts-node-dev) and frontend (Next.js on port 4000, not the default 3000) run independently; backend postinstall runs prisma generate which is required before startup.
nodes.norm_label: dev skill — start dev servers (.claude copy)

nodes.label: new-feature Skill — Scaffold Feature Folder (.claude copy)
nodes.rationale: Scaffolds apps/frontend/app/(app)/<name>/ with page.tsx (layout + data fetching only, ~150 lines max), components/, and hooks/ using TanStack Query — never useEffect for data fetching; asks the user for the data contract before writing real logic.
nodes.norm_label: new-feature skill — scaffold feature folder (.claude copy)

nodes.label: dev Skill — Start Dev Servers (.agents copy)
nodes.norm_label: dev skill — start dev servers (.agents copy)

nodes.label: new-feature Skill — Scaffold Feature Folder (.agents copy)
nodes.norm_label: new-feature skill — scaffold feature folder (.agents copy)

nodes.label: Frontend CI Workflow
nodes.rationale: Runs on PR/push to dev and main: npm ci, lint, tsc --noEmit, vitest, production build, then two security scans; uses public placeholder env values solely to prove the app can build.

nodes.label: CI Step: Reject Legacy Backend Authentication
nodes.rationale: Greps runtime source (app, components, lib, src, proxy.ts) for x-api-key, x-user-id, x-user-email, and the leaked legacy key string to prevent regression to the spoofable header-based auth.
nodes.norm_label: ci step: reject legacy backend authentication

nodes.label: CI Step: Production Bundle Security Scan
nodes.rationale: Scans .next/server and .next/static for forbidden runtime values — legacy auth headers, the leaked key, and localhost:5001 backend URLs — so secrets or dev endpoints cannot ship in the compiled bundle.
nodes.norm_label: ci step: production bundle security scan

nodes.label: SDD Progress Tracker
nodes.rationale: Four tasks marked complete after review; notes the repo lint script is still broken in the Next 16 setup.
